concerns.text: T009 implements a sequential runner using the SAME PerceptionDLM model with context-reset. This violates FR-003 which explicitly mandates executing a 'sequential autoregressive baseline using a standard Transformer-based MLLM (e.g., LLaVA)' to establish ground-truth quality. Using the same model for both parallel and sequential modes changes the experimental design from 'Parallel vs. Sequential Baseline' to 'Parallel vs. Sequential Self', invalidating the comparison required by the spec.

FR-006 and SC-004 require visualizing 'Inference Time' vs. 'Semantic Coherence' to identify a tipping point. T033 attempts to 'append to CSV' time data, but no prior task (T008-T014) implements the actual instrumentation to capture wall-clock inference time for the parallel (T008) or sequential (T009) runners. The data source for the primary visualization is missing from the implementation tasks.

FR-001 and SC-002 require characterizing the degradation curve (linear vs. non-linear) across the 20–50 region range. T004 and T024 restrict generation and testing to only three discrete bins (20, 30, 50). Three data points are insufficient to statistically model non-linearity or define a regression slope as required by SC-002, effectively weakening the requirement to a simple point comparison.

FR-007 mandates Peak RSS < 7 GB RAM with graceful failure or sample reduction if limits are approached. T022 only adds error handling for 'model loading fails'. It lacks the implementation of the runtime memory monitoring logic (reading `/proc/self/status`) and the enforcement mechanism (watchdog) required to trigger the fallback behavior described in the spec's Assumptions.
